summary: DHH celebrates RubyGems reaching 5,000 downloads, calling it one of the best developments in the Ruby ecosystem. He compares it favorably to CPAN and apt-get, noting its rapid growth to 111 gems. He highlights that Rails accounts for at least a quarter of all gem downloads. He advocates for RubyGems to be included in Ruby's standard library and calls on the community to package remaining popular libraries as gems.
key_points: RubyGems reached 5,000 downloads and 20,000 gem retrievals, showing rapid community adoption
Rails was already responsible for at least 25% of all gem downloads, signaling its early dominance
DHH pushed for RubyGems inclusion in Ruby's standard library before the 1.8.2 release
Only three non-gem libraries remained in DHH's setup (FastCGI, MySQL/Ruby, Postgres/Ruby), showing near-complete gem ecosystem coverage
money_quotes: RubyGems is one of the best things that have emerged while I've been Rubying.
It's the equivalent of CPAN + apt-get as a repository for Ruby libraries and applications.
I'm incredibly proud to see that Rails is responsible for at least 1/4 of all gems downloaded.
I can't wait until RubyGems is part of the standard library.
Let's make the world of gems even more exciting.
key_insight: The early success of RubyGems and Rails' outsized share of downloads foreshadowed the package management revolution and Rails' transformative impact on the Ruby ecosystem.
